steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The phrase 'an example of the way in which' demands a choice that shows HOW Ballard blends two traditions in a single work, not one that merely asserts he does so.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: makes a general claim about Ballard's blending without naming both traditions at work in a single piece. ✗
  • Choice B: describes Ballard's background or reputation, not a concrete mechanism of blending. ✗
  • Choice C: states that Ballard combines traditions broadly, but gives no specific composition showing both elements together. ✗
  • Choice D: names Pan Indian Rhythms, identifies it as 'written in a classical form,' and notes it 'incorporates Ute notched sticks' — two traditions functioning together in one work. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
